english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ibn Abī Zā’idah reported from Ḥajjāj from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb from his father from his grandfather from the Prophet ﷺ who said: "If one contracts Mukātabah with his slave for a hundred ounces, and he pays it up to ten ounces, then becomes unable, he is returned to slavery."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: لَا يُرَدُّ حَتَّى يَعْجِزَ عَنْ سِنِينَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Muḥammad ibn Zayd → Abū al-‘Alā’ → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Muḥammad ibn Zayd reported from Abū al-‘Alā’ from Al-Ḥakam who said: "He is not returned until he is unable for years."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا عَلِمْتَ مَكِيلَةَ شَيْءٍ فَلَا تَبِعْهُ جُزَافًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Uyaynah → ‘Amr → Ṭāwūs
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ibn ‘Uyaynah reported from ‘Amr from Ṭāwūs who said: "If you know the measure of something, do not sell it by estimation (Juzāf)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي: الرَّجُلُ يَقُولُ: قَدْ كِلْتُ فِي هَذِهِ الْجَابِيَةِ كَذَا وَكَذَا مَنًا، وَلَا أَدْرِي لَعَلَّهُ يَنْقُصُ أَوْ يَسْرِقُ أَوْ تُشْبِهُ الْجَابِيَةَ، أَوْ كَانَ فِيهِ غَلَطٌ، لَا أَبِيعُكَ كَيْلًا، إِنَّمَا أَبِيعُكَ جُزَافًا قَالَ: كَانَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ يَكْرَهُهُ، وَكَانَ الْحَسَنُ لَا يَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān → His Father
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān said: I said to my father: "A man says: 'I measured in this trough so-and-so many Manns, but I don't know, perhaps it decreased or was stolen, or the trough is similar (to another), or there was a mistake in it. I won't sell it to you by measure, but by estimation.' He said: Ibn Sīrīn used to dislike it, and Al-Ḥasan saw no harm in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ كَالَ جُزَافًا، فَقَالَ لَهُ: مَا فِي بَيْتِكَ كَانَ مِنْ حِنْطَةٍ فَبِكَذَا، وَمَا كَانَ مِنْ شَعِيرٍ فَبِكَذَا وَكَذَا، قَالَ: فَكَرِهَهُ إِبْرَاهِيمُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Jarīr reported from Mughīrah who said: I asked Ibrāhīm about a man who measured by estimation, and said to him: "Whatever wheat was in your house is for such-and-such, and whatever barley is for such-and-such." He said: Ibrāhīm disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ فِطْرٍ، أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ الشَّعْبِيَّ عَنْ قَوْمٍ مِنَ الْأَعْرَابِ يَقْدُمُونَ عَلَيْنَا بِالطَّعَامِ، فَنَشْتَرِي مِنْهُمْ كَيْلًا ثُمَّ نَقُولُ: بِيعُونَا جُزَافًا، قَالَ: لَا، حَتَّى تُتَارَكُوا بِالْبَيْعِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Fiṭr → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Wakī‘ reported from Fiṭr that he asked Al-Sha‘bī about a group of Bedouins who come to us with food, and we buy from them by measure, then we say: "Sell to us by estimation." He said: "No, until you mutually cancel the sale."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَكُنْ يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يَبِيعَهُ جُزَافًا، إِذَا أَعْلَمَهُ أَنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ كَيْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Wakī‘ reported from Sufyān from Ibn Jurayj from ‘Aṭā’ that he saw no harm in selling it by estimation if he informed him that he knows its measure.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوَّادُ بْنُ جَرَّاحٍ أَبُو عِصَامٍ الْعَسْقَلَانِيُّ، عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَسَنَ وَمُجَاهِدًا وَعِكْرِمَةَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَأْتِي الرَّجُلَ، فَابْتَاعَ فِي بَيْتِهِ طَعَامًا فِيهِ مُجَازَفَةً، وَرَبُّ الطَّعَامِ قَدْ عَلِمَ كَيْلَهُ، فَكَرِهَهُ كُلُّهُمْ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Rawwād ibn Jarrāḥ Abū ‘Iṣām al-‘Asqalānī → Al-Awzā‘ī → Yaḥyā ibn Abī Kathīr
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Rawwād ibn Jarrāḥ Abū ‘Iṣām al-‘Asqalānī reported from Al-Awzā‘ī from Yaḥyā ibn Abī Kathīr who said: I asked Al-Ḥasan, Mujāhid, and ‘Ikrimah about a man who comes to another man and buys food in his house by estimation, while the owner of the food knows its measure. They all disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: لَقَدْ رَأَيْتنَا وَفِينَا أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُجَاءُ بِالْأَوْسَاقِ فَتُلْقَى بِالْمُصَلَّى، فَيَقُولُ الرَّجُلُ: كِلْتُ كَذَا وَكَذَا، وَلَا أَبِيعُهُ مُكَايَلَةً، إِنَّمَا أَبِيعُهُ مُجَازَفَةً، فَلَمْ يَرَوْا بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Al-Rabī‘ ibn Nāfi‘
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Wakī‘ reported from Al-Rabī‘ ibn Nāfi‘ who said: We saw amongst us Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when loads were brought and dumped at the prayer place. A man would say: "I measured such-and-such, and I will not sell it by measure, but I will sell it by estimation." They saw no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: كُنَّا نَلْتَقِي بِالرُّكْبَانِ، فَنَشْتَرِي مِنْهُمُ الطَّعَامَ مُجَازَفَةً، فَنَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ نَبِيعَهُ حَتَّى نُحَوِّلَهُ مِنْ مَكَانِهِ أَوْ نَنْقُلَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Alī ibn Mushir → ‘Ubaydullāh → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: ‘Alī ibn Mushir reported from ‘Ubaydullāh from Nāfi‘ from Ibn ‘Umar who said: We used to meet the riders and buy food from them by estimation. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us to sell it until we moved it from its place or transported it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، وَأَشْعَثَ، وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، فِي مُكَاتَبٍ مَاتَ وَعَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِنْ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ، قَالَ: يَضْرِبُ مَوَالِيهِ بِمَا حَلَّ مِنْ نُجُومِهِ. وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ: يَضْرِبُونَ بِمَا حَلَّ مَا لَمْ يَحِلَّ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ → Al-Shaybānī, Ash‘ath, and Ismā‘īl → Al-Sha‘bī → Shurayḥ
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ḥafṣ reported from Al-Shaybānī, Ash‘ath, and Ismā‘īl from Al-Sha‘bī from Shurayḥ regarding a Mukātab who died owing a debt and remaining payments of his Mukātabah. He said: "His masters claim a share equal to the installments that have become due." Ḥammād said: "They claim a share for what is due and what is not due."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: أَخْطَأَ شُرَيْحٌ وَإِنْ كَانَ قَاضِيًا، كَانَ زَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ يَقُولُ: بِالدَّيْنِ قَبْلَ الْمُكَاتَبَةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ḥafṣ reported from Sa‘īd from Qatādah from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib who said: Shurayḥ erred, even though he was a judge. Zayd ibn Thābit used to say: "Debt before Mukātabah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: إِذَا مَاتَ الْمُكَاتَبُ وَعَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ يَضْرِبُ مَوَالِيهِ بِمَا حَلَّ مِنْ نُجُومِهِ مَعَ الْغُرَمَاءِ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ نَجْمٌ حَالٌّ بُدِئَ بِالْغُرَمَاءِ فَأَخَذُوا دَيْنَهُمْ، فَإِنْ فَضَلَ شَيْءٌ كَانَ لِمَوَالِيهِ حَتَّى تَتِمَّ مُكَاتَبَتُهُ، فَإِنْ فَضَلَ شَيْءٌ بَعْدَ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ كَانَ لِوَرَثَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Jarīr reported from Manṣūr from Ibrāhīm who said: "If the Mukātab dies owing a debt, his masters claim a share with the creditors for the installments that have become due. If no installment is due, the creditors are started with and they take their debt. If anything remains, it is for his masters until his Mukātabah is completed. If anything remains after his Mukātabah, it is for his heirs."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يَضْرِبُ مَوْلَاهُ مَعَ الْغُرَمَاءِ بِمَا حَلَّ مِنْ نُجُومِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Wakī‘ reported from Sufyān from Manṣūr from Ibrāhīm who said: "His master claims a share with the creditors for the installments that have become due."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: يُبْدَأُ بِالدَّيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam → Shurayḥ
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ghundar reported from Shu‘bah from Al-Ḥakam from Shurayḥ who said: "It begins with the debt."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وَالْحَكَمِ، أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا: إِذَا كَانَ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الدَّيْنُ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِنْ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ، قَالَ: يُنْظَرُ إِلَى مَا حَلَّ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ نُجُومِهِ، وَمَا كَانَ لِغُرَمَائِهِ، فَيُقَسَّمُ ذَلِكَ بِالْحِصَصِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Fuḍayl → Ash‘ath → Al-Sha‘bī and Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ibn Fuḍayl reported from Ash‘ath from Al-Sha‘bī and Al-Ḥakam regarding a man who has debt and remaining payments of his Mukātabah. He said: "It is looked at what installments have become due and what is owed to his creditors, and that is divided proportionally."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، وَسُفْيَانَ، وَابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى كَانُوا يَقُولُونَ: إِذَا مَاتَ وَعَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ حَلَّ مَا عَلَيْهِ، فَيَضْرِبُ الْمَوْلَى مَعَ الْغُرَمَاءِ بِجَمِيعِ الْمُكَاتَبَةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, Sufyān, and Ibn Abī Laylā
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Wakī‘ reported from Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, Sufyān, and Ibn Abī Laylā that they used to say: "If he dies owing debt, what he owes becomes due, so the master claims a share with the creditors for the entire Mukātabah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: أَخْطَأَ شُرَيْحٌ وَإِنْ كَانَ قَاضِيًا، قَالَ: كَانَ زَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ يَبْدَأُ بِالدَّيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Idrīs → Shu‘bah → Qatādah → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ibn Idrīs reported from Shu‘bah from Qatādah from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib who said: Shurayḥ erred, even though he was a judge. He said: "Zayd ibn Thābit used to begin with the debt."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، قَالَ: يُبْدَأُ بِالدَّيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr ibn Abī Shaybah → ‘Abdullāh ibn Idrīs → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib → Zayd ibn Thābit
english.text: Abū Bakr ibn Abī Shaybah reported: ‘Abdullāh ibn Idrīs reported from Sa‘īd from Qatādah from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib from Zayd ibn Thābit who said: "It begins with the debt."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبِي، أَنَّ نَاسًا مِنْ بَنِي فَهْمٍ خَاصَمُوا نَاسًا مِنْ بَنِي سُلَيْمٍ فِي مَعْدِنٍ لَهُمْ إِلَى مَرْوَانَ، فَأَمَرَ مَرْوَانُ ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ أَنْ يَقْضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ، فَاسْتَوَتِ الشُّهُودُ فَأَقْرَعَ بَيْنَهُمْ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ، فَجَعَلَهُ لِمَنْ أَصَابَتْهُ الْقُرْعَةُ مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّ الشُّهُودَ اسْتَوَتْ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → His Father
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Abū Usāmah reported from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah who said: My father informed me that people from Banū Fahm disputed with people from Banū Sulaym regarding a mine of theirs before Marwān. Marwān ordered Ibn al-Zubayr to judge between them. The witnesses were equal, so ‘Abdullāh cast lots between them and assigned it to the one whom the lot favored because the witnesses were equal.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، وَابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا اسْتَوَتِ الْبَيِّنَتَانِ فَهِيَ لِلَّذِي فِي أَيْدِيهِمْ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar and Ibn Abī Zā’idah → Ḥajjāj → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar and Ibn Abī Zā’idah reported from Ḥajjāj from Ḥammād from Ibrāhīm who said: "If the two proofs are equal, it belongs to the one who possesses it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: فِي الْقَوْمِ إِذَا اخْتَلَفَتْ شَهَادَتُهُمْ وَاسْتَوَوْا فِي التَّعْدِيلِ وَالْعَدَدِ فَالْيَمِينُ عَلَى مَنِ ادَّعَى عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā → Ibn Abī Dhi’b → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā reported from Ibn Abī Dhi’b from Al-Zuhrī that he said regarding people whose testimonies differ but are equal in integrity and number: "The oath is upon the defendant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا تَسْتَقْبِلُوا، وَلَا تُحَفِّلُوا، وَلَا يُنَفِّقْ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Simāk → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Abū Bakr reported: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ reported from Simāk from ‘Ikrimah who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Do not intercept [riders/goods before market], do not retain milk in udders [to deceive], and do not bid up prices against one another."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ الرَّازِيِّ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنْ لَا تَلَقُّوا الْبُيُوعَ بِأَفْوَاهِ السِّكَكِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mubarak → Abu Ja'far al-Razi → Layth → Mujahid → Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mubarak told us from Abu Ja'far al-Razi, from Layth, from Mujahid, from Ibn 'Umar: "Do not meet the merchandise at the entrances of the roads."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ إِيَاسِ بْنِ دَغْفَلٍ، قَالَ: قُرِئَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَابُ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ: لَا تَلَقُّوا الرُّكْبَانَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi → Iyas ibn Daghfal → 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi told us from Iyas ibn Daghfal, who said: The letter of 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz was read to us: "Do not meet the riders."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: نَهَى عَنْ تَلَقِّي الْجَلَبَ، فَإِنْ تَلَقَّى رَجُلٌ، فَاشْتَرَاهُ فَصَاحِبُهُ بِالْخِيَارِ إِذَا قَدِمَ الْمِصْرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad ibn Abi 'Adi → Ibn 'Awn → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Muhammad ibn Abi 'Adi told us from Ibn 'Awn, from Muhammad, who said: "He forbade meeting the imported goods. If a man meets [them] and buys, then its owner has the option [to annul] when he reaches the market."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ النَّهْدِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهُ نَهَى عَنْ تَلَقِّي الْبُيُوعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mubarak → Al-Taymi → Abu 'Uthman al-Nahdi → Ibn Mas'ud
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mubarak told us from Al-Taymi, from Abu 'Uthman al-Nahdi, from Ibn Mas'ud, from the Prophet (saw): "That he forbade meeting the merchandise."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا تَلَقُّوا الرُّكْبَانَ لِلْبَيْعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Muhammad ibn 'Amr → Abu Salamah → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Muhammad ibn 'Amr, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah (saw) said: "Do not meet the riders for sale."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ نَوْفَلِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهُ نَهَى عَنِ التَّلَقِّي

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Ubayd Allah → Al-Rabi' ibn Habib → Nawfal ibn 'Abd al-Malik → His Father → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Ubayd Allah told us from Al-Rabi' ibn Habib, from Nawfal ibn 'Abd al-Malik, from his father, from 'Ali: "That the Prophet (saw) forbade meeting [merchandise before it reaches the market]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَا: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ تُلَقَّى الْبُيُوعُ مِنْ أَفْوَاهِ الطُّرُقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Layth → Mujahid → Ibn 'Umar/Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from Layth, from Mujahid, from Ibn 'Umar and Abu Hurayrah, who said: "The Messenger of Allah (saw) forbade meeting the merchandise at the entrances of the roads."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُسْتَكْرِي، وَالْمُسْتَعِيرِ، وَالْمُسْتَوْدَعِ ضَمَانٌ إِلَّا أَنْ يُخَالِفَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "There is no liability on the lessee, the borrower, and the depositary unless he contravenes [the agreement]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ الشَّعْبِيَّ، يَقُولُ: إِذَا خَالَفَ الْمُسْتَوْدَعُ، وَالْمُسْتَعِيرُ، وَالْمُسْتَبْضَعُ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Isma'il ibn Salim → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Isma'il ibn Salim, who said: I heard Al-Sha'bi say: "If the depositary, the borrower, or the one given goods to sell contravenes [the agreement], he is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا نَهَيْتَ مُضَارِبَكَ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَ مِنْ مَتَاعِ كَذَا وَكَذَا، فَاشْتَرَى ضَمِنَ وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ: يَتَصَدَّقَانِ بِالرِّبْحِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If you forbid your Mudarib (speculator) from buying such and such goods, and he buys [them], he is liable." And Hammad said: "They give the profit in charity."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، قَالَ: الْمُضَارِبُ مُؤْتَمَنٌ، وَإِنْ خَالَفَ أَمْرَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman → Layth → Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman told us from Layth, from Tawus, who said: "The Mudarib is trusted, even if he opposes your command."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ ضَمَّنَ أَنَسًا أَرْبَعَةَ آلَافٍ كَانَتْ مَعَهُ مُضَارَبَةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idris → Al-Shaybani → Al-Sha'bi → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Idris told us from Al-Shaybani, from Al-Sha'bi, that 'Umar held Anas liable for four thousand that were with him as Mudarabah.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: الْمُضَارِبُ مُؤْتَمَنٌ، وَإِنْ خَالَفَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu'tamir → Humayd → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Mu'tamir told us from Humayd, from Al-Hasan, who said: "The Mudarib is trusted, even if he contravenes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: "اسْتُودِعْتُ سِتَّةَ آلَافٍ فَذَهَبَتْ، فَقَالَ لِي عُمَرُ:" ذَهَبَ لَكَ مَعَهَا شَيْءٌ؟ قُلْتُ: لَا، قَالَ: فَضَمَّنَنِي

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idris → Hisham → Anas ibn Sirin → Anas ibn Malik → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Idris told us from Hisham, from Anas ibn Sirin, from Anas ibn Malik, who said: "I was entrusted with six thousand, and it was lost. 'Umar said to me: 'Did anything of yours get lost with it?' I said: No. He said: So he held me liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُهَزَّمِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: "إِذَا شَرَطَ رَبُّ الْمَالِ عَلَى الْمُضَارِبِ: لَا يَنْزِلُ بَطْنَ وَادٍ، فَنَزَلَ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Hammad ibn Salamah → Abu al-Muhazzam → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Abu al-Muhazzam, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: "If the owner of the capital stipulates to the Mudarib not to descend into a valley, and he descends, then he is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: مَنْ قَاسَمَ الرِّبْحَ فَلَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Shu'bah → Qatadah → 'Abd Allah ibn al-Harith → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Shu'bah, from Qatadah, from 'Abd Allah ibn al-Harith, from 'Ali, who said: "Whoever shares the profit is not liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ فِي مُضَارِبٍ دَفَعَ الْمَالَ إِلَى غَيْرِهِ، قَالَ: لَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ، هُوَ أَمِينٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mansur → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Mansur, from Al-Hasan, regarding a Mudarib who gave the money to someone else. He said: "He is not liable; he is trustworthy."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ ابْنِ أَخِي شُرَيْحٍ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: إِذَا خَالَفَ فِي الْوَدِيعَةِ وَالْكَرِيِّ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam → Hajjaj → 'Uthman ibn Akhi Shurayh
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam told us from Hajjaj, from 'Uthman the nephew of Shurayh, that he said: "If he contravenes regarding the deposit or the rental, he is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ فِي مُضَارِبٍ قَالَ لَهُ صَاحِبُ الْمَالِ: لَا تُجَاوِزْ مَكَانَ كَذَا وَكَذَا، قَالَ: ضَمِنَ إِنْ جَاوَزَهُ.

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Dawud → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Dawud, from Al-Sha'bi regarding a Mudarib whom the owner of the capital told: Do not cross such and such place. He said: "He is liable if he crosses it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، بِنَحْوِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Ali ibn Mushir → Dawud → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Ali ibn Mushir told us from Dawud, from Al-Sha'bi, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: لَا تَشْتَرِطُ عَلَى الْمُضَارِبِ شَيْئًا، فَإِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يُخَالِفَ فَيُفْسِدَ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَى نَفْسِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Ash'ath → Ibn Sirin
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Ash'ath, from Ibn Sirin, who said: "Do not impose conditions on the Mudarib, for I fear he may contravene and spoil it for you and himself."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ دَفَعَ إِلَيْهِ مَالَهُ مُضَارَبَةً، وَقَالَ: لَا تَخْرُجْ مِنَ الْمِصْرِ، فَخَرَجَ، قَالَ: لَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, that he was asked about a man who gave his money to him as Mudarabah and said: Do not leave the city, but he left. He said: "There is no liability on him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، فِي الْمُضَارِبِ إِذَا اشْتَرَطُوا عَلَيْهِ لَا يُجَاوِزَ، فَجَاوَزَ، فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi → Khalid → Abu Qilabah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi told us from Khalid, from Abu Qilabah, regarding the Mudarib: If they stipulate that he should not cross [a limit], and he crosses, then he is liable.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ إِيَاسِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ، قَالَ: هُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-Wahhab → Ayyub → Iyas ibn Mu'awiyah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-Wahhab told us from Ayyub, from Iyas ibn Mu'awiyah, who said: "He is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: إِنْ نَهَاهُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ، فَخَرَجَ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → 'Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from 'Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman, from 'Ata', who said: "If he forbade him from going out, and he went out, then he is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، وَمُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا خَالَفَ الْمُسْتَوْدِعَ، وَالْمُسْتَعِيرَ، وَالْمُسْتَبْضِعَ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Husayn / Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Husayn and Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If the depositary, the borrower, or the one given goods to sell contravenes [the agreement], he is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ: أَنَّ أَبَا بَكْرٍ، كَانَ لَا يُضَمِّنُ الْوَدِيعَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Hajjaj → Abu al-Zubayr → Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs told us from Hajjaj, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir: That Abu Bakr did not hold the depositary liable.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُكَيْمٍ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا اسْتَوْدَعَ رَجُلًا وَدِيعَةً فَهَلَكَتْ، فَلَمْ يُضَمِّنُهُ عُمَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Hajjaj → Hilal ibn 'Abd al-Rahman → 'Abd Allah ibn 'Ukaym → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar told us from Hajjaj, from Hilal ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from 'Abd Allah ibn 'Ukaym, that a man entrusted another man with a deposit and it perished, so 'Umar did not hold him liable.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُسْتَوْدِعِ وَالْمُسْتَعِيرِ ضَمَانٌ إِلَّا أَنْ يُتَّهَمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, who said: "There is no liability on the depositary and the borrower unless he is suspected."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، وَعَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا لَا يَرَيَانِ بَأْسًا بِالرَّهْنِ إِذَا كَانَ عَلَى يَدَيْ عَدْلٍ مَقْبُوضًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughirah / Ash'ath → Al-Harith / Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Mughirah, from Al-Harith, and from Ash'ath, from Al-Hakam, that they saw no harm in the pledge if it was in the hands of a just person and possessed.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، وَأَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ قَالَ: هُوَ رَهْنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Jabir / Ash'ath → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Jabir and Ash'ath, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "It is a pledge."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: لَا يَكُونُ رَهْنُهُ حَتَّى يَقْبِضَهُ صَاحِبُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Ash'ath → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hakam, who said: "It is not a pledge until its owner takes possession of it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ أَنَّهُ قَرَأَهَا: {فَرِهَانٌ مَقْبُوضَةٌ} [البقرة: 283] قَالَ: لَا يَكُونُ الرَّهْنُ إِلَّا مَقْبُوضًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sharik → Salim → Sa'id
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sharik, from Salim, from Sa'id, that he recited: {Then a pledge taken} [Al-Baqarah: 283], and said: "A pledge is not valid unless taken into possession."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُعْطِيَ الرَّجُلُ مَالًا مُضَارَبَةً عَلَى أَنْ يُعْطِيَهُ بِضَاعَةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, that he disliked for a man to be given money as Mudarabah on the condition that he gives him goods.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي رَوَّادٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَرِهَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Rawwad → Ma'mar → Ibn Tawus → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Rawwad told us from Ma'mar, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, that he disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ دَفَعَ إِلَى رَجُلٍ مَالًا مُضَارَبَةً، وَاشْتَرَطَ عَلَيْهِ بِضَاعَةً أَنَّهُ كَرِهَهُ، وَكَانَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ: لَا يَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Ash'ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ghundar told us from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan, that he was asked about a man who gave money to another as Mudarabah and stipulated goods upon him, and he disliked it. And Ibn Sirin saw no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يَدْفَعَ الرَّجُلُ إِلَى الرَّجُلِ مَالًا مُضَارَبَةً عَلَى أَنْ يَحْمِلَ لَهُ بِضَاعَةً أَوْ يَعْمَلَ لَهُ عَمَلًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn 'Ulayyah → Ayyub → Muhammad ibn Sirin
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us from Ayyub, from Muhammad ibn Sirin, that he saw no harm in a man giving money to another as Mudarabah on the condition that he carries goods for him or does some work for him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ فِي أُمِّ الْوَلَدِ: أَعْتَقَهَا وَلَدُهَا، وَإِنْ كَانَ سِقْطًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → His Father → 'Ikrimah → 'Umar ibn al-Khattab
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from his father, from 'Ikrimah, who said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab said regarding the Umm al-Walad: "Her child has freed her, even if it was a miscarriage."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ ذَرٍّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ قَارِبٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ اشْتَرَى مِنْ رَجُلٍ جَارِيَةً بِأَرْبَعَةِ آلَافٍ قَدْ كَانَتْ أَسْقَطَتْ مِنْ مَوْلَاهَا سِقْطًا، فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ عُمَرَ، فَأَتَاهُ فَعَلَاهُ بِالدِّرَّةِ ضَرْبًا، وَقَالَ: بَعْدَمَا اخْتَلَطَتْ لُحُومُكُمْ بِلُحُومِهِنَّ، وَدِمَاؤُكُمْ بِدِمَائِهِنَّ بِعْتُمُوهُنَّ، لَعَنَ اللَّهُ الْيَهُودَ، حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الشُّحُومَ، فَبَاعُوهَا وَأَكَلُوا أَثْمَانَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → 'Umar ibn Dharr → Muhammad ibn 'Abd Allah ibn Qarib al-Thaqafi → His Father → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from 'Umar ibn Dharr, from Muhammad ibn 'Abd Allah ibn Qarib al-Thaqafi, from his father, that he bought a slave girl for four thousand from a man, who had miscarried a fetus from her master. This reached 'Umar, so he came to him and struck him with a whip, saying: "After your flesh mixed with their flesh and your blood with their blood, you sold them? May Allah curse the Jews; fat was forbidden to them, so they sold it and ate its price."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا حَمَلَتِ الْأَمَةُ مِنْ سَيِّدِهَا، ثُمَّ أَسْقَطَتْ، قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ اسْتَبَانَ خَلْقَهُ فَهِيَ أُمُّ وَلَدٍ، لَا سَبِيلَ إِلَى بَيْعِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: If a slave girl becomes pregnant by her master and then miscarries, he said: "If its form was distinct, she is an Umm al-Walad, and there is no way to sell her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا انْكَسَرَ فِي الْخَلْقِ الرَّابِعِ، فَكَانَ مُخَلَّقًا أُعْتِقَتْ بِهِ الْأَمَةُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Dawud → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Dawud, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "If it breaks in the fourth creation and is formed, the slave girl is freed by it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدٍ، أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا: إِذَا أَسْقَطَتِ الْأَمَةُ مِنْ سَيِّدِهَا فَهِيَ حُرَّةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Hisham → Al-Hasan / Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan and Muhammad, who said: "If the slave girl miscarries from her master, she is free."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: تُعْتَقُ أُمُّ الْوَلَدِ إِذَا أَسْقَطَتْ إِذَا عُلِمَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ سِقْطًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ma'n ibn 'Isa → Ibn Abi Dhi'b → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ma'n ibn 'Isa told us from Ibn Abi Dhi'b, from Al-Zuhri, who said: "The Umm al-Walad is freed if she miscarries, provided it is known to be a miscarriage."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، فِي أُمِّ الْوَلَدِ إِذَا وَضَعَتْهُ وَهُوَ مُضْغَةٌ فَقَدْ عَتَقَتْ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yazid ibn Harun → Hammad ibn Salamah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yazid ibn Harun told us from Hammad ibn Salamah regarding the Umm al-Walad: "If she delivers it as a clot, she is freed by it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ رُفَيْعٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ قُلْتُ: إِنَّا نَحْمِلُ هَذِهِ الْبَضَائِعَ لِلنَّاسِ فَنَحْتَاجُ إِلَيْهَا فِي الطَّرِيقِ، قَالَ: إِذَا قَدِمَتِ اشْتَرَيْتُ لِأَصْحَابِهَا حَاجَتَهَا وَلَمْ تَحْبِسْهَا قُلْتُ: بَلَى، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ، هُوَ خَيْرٌ لِصَاحِبِ الْبِضَاعَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Rufay' → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Rufay', from 'Ata', who said: I asked him, saying: We carry these goods for people and we need them on the way. He said: "If you arrive, you buy for its owners what is needed and do not withhold it?" I said: Yes. He said: "There is no harm; it is better for the owner of the goods."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ دُفِعَتْ إِلَيْهِ دَرَاهِمُ يَشْتَرِي بِهَا شَيْئًا، فَصَرَفَهَا فِي حَاجَتِهِ، ثُمَّ رَدَّهَا، فَاشْتَرَى بِهَا الَّذِي أُمِرَ بِهِ، قَالَ: هُوَ ضَامِنٌ حَتَّى يُسَلِّمَهَا إِلَى رَبِّهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yazid ibn Harun → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yazid ibn Harun told us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who was given dirhams to buy something, but he spent them on his needs, then returned them and bought what he was ordered to buy. He said: "He is liable until he hands it over to its owner."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ مَاهَانَ، قَالَ: مَرَّ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ عَلَى رَجُلٍ يَزِنُ دُوَيْرَةً قَدْ أَرْجَحَ، فَقَالَ: أَقِمْ لِسَانَ الْمِيزَانِ، فَإِذَا اسْتَقَامَ فَزِدْهُ مِنْ مَالِكٍ مَا شِئْتَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Ali ibn Mushir → Isma'il → Mahan → Ibn Mas'ud
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Ali ibn Mushir told us from Isma'il, from Mahan, who said: Ibn Mas'ud passed by a man weighing a small weight and he tipped the scale. He said: "Straighten the tongue of the scale, and when it is straight, add from your own wealth whatever you wish."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي سِنَانٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْهُذَيْلِ، كَذَا قَالَ أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَمَّارَ بْنَ يَاسِرٍ اشْتَرَى قِثَّاءً فَاسْتَزَادَهُ حَبْلًا فَأَبَى أَنْ يَزِيدَهُ، فَرَأَيْتُ عَمَّارًا يُنَازِعُهُ إِيَّاهُ، فَلَا أَدْرِي أَيُّهُمَا غَلَبَ عَلَيْهِ.

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Abu Sinan → Abu al-Hudhayl → 'Ammar ibn Yasir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu al-Ahwas told us from Abu Sinan, from Abu al-Hudhayl - as Abu al-Ahwas said - who said: "I saw 'Ammar ibn Yasir buying cucumbers and he asked for an extra bundle, but he refused to give him more. I saw 'Ammar disputing with him over it, and I do not know which of them prevailed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سِنَانٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي الْهُذَيْلِ، عَنْ عَمَّارٍ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Abu Sinan → Ibn Abi al-Hudhayl → 'Ammar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Muhammad ibn Fudayl told us from Abu Sinan, from Ibn Abi al-Hudhayl, from 'Ammar, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنْ بَهْدَلِ أَبِي الْوَضَّاحِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: أَنَّهُ مَرَّ عَلَى عَائِشَةَ وَقَدِ اشْتَرَتْ لَحْمًا وَهِيَ تَقُولُ: زِدْنِي، فَقَالَ لَهُ: زِدْهَا، هُوَ أَعْظَمُ لِبَرَكَةِ الْبَيْعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Yunus → Bahdal Abu al-Waddah al-Shaybani → A man → 'Ali / 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Yunus, from Bahdal Abu al-Waddah al-Shaybani, from a man, from 'Ali: That he passed by 'Aishah who had bought meat and was saying: "Give me more." He said to him: "Increase for her; it is greater for the blessing of the sale."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَسْتَزِيدَ عَلَى الْبَيْعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Isra'il → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Isra'il, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "There is no harm in asking for an increase on the sale."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِي حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنَ النَّخَعِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَمَّارًا اشْتَرَى قِثَّاءً مِنْ رَجُلٍ فَنَازَعَهُ حَبْلًا، وَعَمَّارٌ يَقُولُ: زِدْنِي وَالْآخَرُ يَقُولُ: لَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Abu Husayn → A man from Al-Nakha' → 'Ammar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us: Sufyan told us from Abu Husayn, from a man of Al-Nakha', who said: "I saw 'Ammar buying cucumbers from a man and disputing with him over a bundle." 'Ammar was saying: "Give me more," and the other was saying: "No."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَمَّنْ سَمِعَ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، يَقُولُ لَهُ: إِذَا اشْتَرَيْتَ لَحْمًا فَلَا تَسْتَزِدْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Jabir → Someone who heard → Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Jabir, from someone who heard Ibn 'Umar saying to him: "If you buy meat, do not ask for more."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو الشَّعْثَاءِ: لَا يَجُوزُ لِامْرَأَةٍ عَطِيَّةً حَتَّى تَلِدَ شَرْوَاهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn 'Uyaynah → 'Amr → Abu al-Sha'tha'
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn 'Uyaynah told us from 'Amr, who said: Abu al-Sha'tha' said: "A gift is not valid for a woman until she gives birth to her like."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: لَا يَجُوزُ لِامْرَأَةٍ عَطِيَّةٌ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ زَوْجِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn 'Uyaynah → Ibn Tawus → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn 'Uyaynah told us from Ibn Tawus, from his father, who said: "A gift is not valid for a woman except with her husband's permission."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا حَالَتْ فِي بَيْتِهَا حَوْلًا جَازَ لَهَا مَا صَنَعَتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Mughirah → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu al-Ahwas told us from Mughirah, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "If a year passes while she is in her house, what she did becomes valid for her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا وَلَدَتِ الْجَارِيَةُ أَوْ وَلَدَ مِثْلُهَا جَازَ لَهَا هِبَتُهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu al-Ahwas told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If the slave girl gives birth or one like her gives birth, her gift becomes valid."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: عَهِدَ إِلَيَّ عُمَرُ أَنْ لَا أُجِيزَ هِبَةً مُمَلَّكَةً حَتَّى تَحُولَ فِي بَيْتِهَا حَوْلًا أَوْ تَلِدَ بَطْنًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → His Father → 'Amir → Shurayh → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from his father, from 'Amir, from Shurayh, who said: "'Umar covenanted with me not to validate a gift of a possessed woman until a year passes in her house or she gives birth."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: قَرَأْتُ كِتَابَ عُمَرَ إِلَى شُرَيْحٍ بِذَلِكَ: وَذَلِكَ أَنَّ جَارِيَةً مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ قَالَ لَهَا أَخُوهَا وَهِيَ مُمَلَّكَةٌ: تَصَدَّقِي عَلَيَّ بِمِيرَاثِكِ مِنْ أَبِيكِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَذْهَبِي إِلَى زَوْجِكِ، فَفَعَلَتْ، ثُمَّ طَلَبَتْ مِيرَاثَهَا فَرَدَّهُ عَلَيْهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Mujalid → 'Amir → 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Mujalid, from 'Amir, who said: I read 'Umar's letter to Shurayh regarding that: A slave girl from Quraysh was told by her brother while she was possessed: "Give me your inheritance from your father as charity before you go to your husband." She did so, then she demanded her inheritance, and he returned it to her.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ مُحَمَّدٌ: لَا تَجُوزُ لِامْرَأَةٍ عَطِيَّةٌ حَتَّى تَحُولَ حَوْلًا أَوْ تَلِدَ وَلَدًا، وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: حَتَّى تَلِدَ وَلَدًا أَوْ تَبْلُغَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi → Sa'id ibn 'Abd al-Rahman → Al-Hasan / Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi told us from Sa'id ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, from Al-Hasan and Muhammad. Muhammad said: "A gift is not valid for a woman until a year passes or she gives birth to a child." Al-Hasan said: "Until she gives birth to a child or reaches that stage."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ عَنَسَتْ قَالَ: لَا يَجُوزُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Isma'il, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: I said to him: "What if she remains unmarried (becomes an old maid)?" He said: "It is not valid."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَا لِلْيَتِيمَةِ خِنَاقَانِ: لَا يَجُوزُ لَهَا شَيْءٌ فِي مَالِهَا حَتَّى تَلِدَ وَلَدًا أَوْ تَمْضِي عَلَيْهَا سَنَةٌ فِي بَيْتِ زَوْجِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Ubayd Allah → 'Uthman ibn al-Aswad → 'Ata' / Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Ubayd Allah told us from 'Uthman ibn al-Aswad, from 'Ata' and Mujahid, who said regarding the female orphan: “She has two constrictions: Nothing is valid for her regarding her wealth until she gives birth to a child or a year passes while she is in her husband's house.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، وَزَكَرِيَّا، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: عَهِدَ إِلَيَّ عُمَرُ أَنْ لَا أُجِيزَ هِبَةَ جَارِيَةٍ حَتَّى تَحُولَ فِي بَيْتِهَا حَوْلًا أَوْ تَلِدَ وَلَدًا. قَالَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ: قُلْتُ لِلشَّعْبِيِّ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ عَنَسَتْ يَجُوزُ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Isma'il / Zakariyya → Al-Sha'bi → Shurayh
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Isma'il and Zakariyya, from Al-Sha'bi, from Shurayh, who said: “'Umar instructed me not to validate a gift of a slave girl until a year passes for her in her house or she gives birth to a child.” Isma'il said: I said to Al-Sha'bi: “What if she becomes an old maid, is it valid?” He said: “Yes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا بِثَمَنِ الْهِرِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idris → Hisham → Ibn Sirin
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Idris told us from Hisham, from Ibn Sirin, who said: “He used to see no harm in the price of a cat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، أَنَّهُمَا كَرِهَا ثَمَنَ السِّنَّوْرِ، وَبَيْعَهُ وَأَكْلَ لَحْمِهِ، وَأَنْ يُنْتَفَعَ بِجِلْدِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Layth → Mujahid / Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs told us from Layth, from Mujahid and Tawus, that they disliked the price of a cat, selling it, eating its meat, and benefiting from its skin.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، وَحَمَّادًا عَنْ ثَمَنِ السِّنَّوْرِ، فَقَالَا: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idris → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam / Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Idris told us from Shu'bah, who said: I asked Al-Hakam and Hammad about the price of a cat, and they said: “There is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي حُرَّةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ اشْتَرَى هِرًّا، فَقَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِشِرَائِهِ، كُرِهَ ثَمَنُهُ لِلْبَائِعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi → Abu Hurrah → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi told us from Abu Hurrah, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who bought a cat. He said: “There is no harm in buying it, but its price is disliked for the seller.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً عَنْهُ، فَقَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Abu Hanifah → 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Abu Hanifah, who said: I asked 'Ata' about it, and he said: “There is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، قَالَ: أَرَى أَنَّ أَبَا سُفْيَانَ، ذَكَرَهُ عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ ثَمَنِ الْهِرِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Al-A'mash → Abu Sufyan → Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Al-A'mash, who said: I think Abu Sufyan mentioned it from Jabir, who said: “The Messenger of Allah (saw) forbade the price of a cat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُهَزَّمِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، وَعَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ أَنَّهُمَا كَرِهَا ثَمَنَ الْهِرِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Hammad ibn Salamah → Abu al-Muhazzam / Abu al-Zubayr → Abu Hurayrah / Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Abu al-Muhazzam, from Abu Hurayrah, and from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, that they both disliked the price of a cat.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ قَابُوسِ بْنِ أَبِي الْمُخَارِقِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: بَعَثَ عَلِيٌّ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ أَبِي بَكْرٍ عَلَى مِصْرَ، فَكَتَبَ إِلَيْهِ يَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ مُكَاتَبٍ مَاتَ وَتَرَكَ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا، فَكَتَبَ يَأْمُرُ فِي الْكِتَابِ: إِنْ كَانَ تَرَكَ وَفَاءً لِمُكَاتَبَتِهِ يَدَّعِي مَوَالِيهِ فَيَسْتَوْفُونَ، وَمَا بَقِيَ كَانَ مِيرَاثًا لِوَلَدِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Simak → Qabus ibn Abi al-Mukhariq → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu al-Ahwas told us from Simak, from Qabus ibn Abi al-Mukhariq, from his father, who said: 'Ali sent Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr to Egypt. He wrote to him asking about a Mukatab (slave with a contract of manumission) who died leaving wealth and children. He wrote ordering in the letter: “If he left enough to fulfill his Mukatabah, his masters claim it and receive full payment, and whatever remains is inheritance for his children.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: إِنَّ شُرَيْحًا كَانَ يَقْضِي فِي الْمُكَاتَبِ يَمُوتُ وَيَتْرُكُ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا، يُؤَدِّي عَنْهُ لِمَوَالِيهِ مَا بَقِيَ مِنْ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ، وَمَا بَقِيَ رَدَّهُ عَلَى وَلَدِهِ؟ فَقَالَ: إِنَّ شُرَيْحًا كَانَ يَقْضِي فِيهَا بِقَضَاءِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Isma'il, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: I said to him: Shurayh used to judge regarding a Mukatab who dies leaving wealth and children, that what remains of his Mukatabah is paid to his masters on his behalf, and what is left is returned to his children? He said: Shurayh used to judge in it according to the judgment of 'Abd Allah.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِنْ فَضَلَ شَيْءٌ كَانَ لِمَوَالِيهِ حَتَّى تَتِمَّ مُكَاتَبَتُهُ، وَإِنْ فَضَلَ شَيْءٌ بَعْدَ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ كَانَ لِوَرَثَتِهِ.

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: “If anything remains, it belongs to his masters until his Mukatabah is completed. If anything remains after his Mukatabah, it belongs to his heirs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ، وَزَيْدَ بْنَ ثَابِتٍ قَالَا: إِذَا مَاتَ الْمُكَاتَبُ وَلَهُ مَالٌ فَهُوَ لِمَوَالِيهِ، وَلَيْسَ لِوَلَدِهِ شَيْءٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad ibn Yazid → Abu al-'Ala' → Qatadah → Umar ibn al-Khattab / Zayd ibn Thabit
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Muhammad ibn Yazid told us from Abu al-'Ala', from Qatadah, that 'Umar ibn al-Khattab and Zayd ibn Thabit said: “If the Mukatab dies and has wealth, it belongs to his masters, and his children have nothing.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، فِي مُكَاتَبٍ مَاتَ وَتَرَكَ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا أَحْرَارًا قَالَ: يُؤَدِّي مَا بَقِيَ مِنْ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ، وَمَا بَقِيَ فَلِوَلَدِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid → 'Amir → 'Abd Allah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid, from 'Amir, from 'Abd Allah, regarding a Mukatab who died leaving wealth and free children. He said: “What remains of his Mukatabah is paid, and what remains is for his children.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ، "أَعْتَقَهُ، فَقَالَ: أَمَا إِنَّ مَالَكَ لِي، وَلَكِنَّهُ لَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Maysarah → His Father → His Grandfather → 'Abd Allah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Sharik told us from Maysarah, from his father, from his grandfather, that 'Abd Allah freed him and said: “Indeed, your wealth belongs to me, but it is for you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّ أَنَسًا، سَأَلَ غُلَامًا لَهُ عَنْ مَالِهِ، فَأَخْبَرَهُ فَقَالَ: أَنْتَ وَمَالُكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Isma'il ibn Ibrahim → Ayyub → Ibn Sirin → Anas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Isma'il ibn Ibrahim told us from Ayyub, from Ibn Sirin, that Anas asked a slave of his about his wealth. He informed him, so he said: “You and your wealth [are free].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ، أَنَّ امْرَأَةً مِنْ قَوْمِ عَائِشَةَ أَعْتَقَتْ مَمْلُوكًا، فَسَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ، فَقَالَتْ: إِذَا أَعْتَقْتِيهِ وَلَمْ تَشْتَرِطِي مَالَهُ فَمَالُهُ لَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Hisham al-Dastuwa'i → Abu al-Zubayr → 'Abd Allah ibn Abi Mulaykah → 'Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ghundar told us from Hisham al-Dastuwa'i, from Abu al-Zubayr, from 'Abd Allah ibn Abi Mulaykah, that a woman from 'Aishah's people freed a slave. I asked 'Aishah, and she said: “If you freed him and did not stipulate his wealth, then his wealth belongs to him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَنْبَسِ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَعْتَقَ غُلَامًا لَهُ، فَقَالَ: أَمَا إِنَّ الْمَالَ لِي، وَلَكِنَّهُ لَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Abu al-'Anbas → 'Imran ibn 'Umayr → His Father → 'Abd Allah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Abu al-'Anbas, from 'Imran ibn 'Umayr, from his father, from 'Abd Allah, who freed a slave of his and said: “Indeed, the wealth belongs to me, but it is for you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّ أَبَا أَيُّوبَ، دَعَا غُلَامًا لَهُ فَسَأَلَهُ عَنْ مَالِهِ فَأَخْبَرَهُ فَقَالَ: أَنْتَ وَمَالُكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Hisham → Ibn Sirin → Abu Ayyub
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Hisham, from Ibn Sirin, that Abu Ayyub called a slave of his and asked him about his wealth. He informed him, so he said: “You and your wealth [are free].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا أُعْتِقَ الْعَبْدُ تَبِعَهُ مَالُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Ali ibn Mushir → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Ali ibn Mushir told us from Isma'il, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: “If a slave is freed, his wealth follows him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: الْمَالُ لِلْعَبْدِ إِلَّا أَنْ يَسْتَثْنِيَهُ السَّيِّدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: “The wealth belongs to the slave unless the master stipulates it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَعْتَقَ الرَّجُلُ غُلَامَهُ تَبِعَهُ مَالُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu'awiyah → Al-A'mash → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Mu'awiyah told us from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, who said: “If a man frees his slave, his wealth follows him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَعْتَقَ الرَّجُلُ الْعَبْدَ وَلَهُ مَالٌ فَمَالُ الْعَبْدِ لِلْعَبْدِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman → Hasan ibn Salih → Ibn Abi Najih → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman told us from Hasan ibn Salih, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, who said: “If a man frees a slave and he has wealth, the slave's wealth belongs to the slave.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا أُعْتِقَ الْعَبْدُ تَبِعَهُ مَالُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Layth → Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Layth, from Tawus, who said: “If a slave is freed, his wealth follows him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، فِي الَّذِي يَعْتِقُ الْعَبْدَ وَلَهُ مَالٌ، قَالَ: أُحِبُّ أَنْ يُبَيِّنَ لَهُ، إِنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يُمْسِكَهُ مَسَكَهُ، وَإِنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَجْعَلَهُ مَعَهُ جَعَلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad ibn Abi 'Adi → Ibn 'Awn → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Muhammad ibn Abi 'Adi told us from Ibn 'Awn, from Muhammad, regarding one who frees a slave who has wealth. He said: “I like that he clarifies it to him; if he wants to keep it, he keeps it, and if he wants to let him have it, he lets him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَعْتَقَ الْعَبْدَ مَمْلُوكُهُ، وَلَهُ مَالٌ فَمَالُهُ مَمْلُوكُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, who said: “If a slave frees his slave, and he has wealth, his wealth belongs to his slave.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَعْتَقَهُ فَالْمَالُ لِلسَّيِّدِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, who said: “If he frees him, the wealth belongs to the master.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ أَبِي عَوْنٍ الثَّقَفِيِّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، وَعَلِيٍّ، قَالَا: إِذَا أَسْلَمَ، وَلَهُ أَرْضٌ وَضَعْنَا عَنْهُ الْجِزْيَةَ، وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُ خَرَاجَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Muhammad ibn Qays → Muhammad ibn 'Ubayd Allah Abu 'Awn al-Thaqafi → 'Umar / 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs told us from Muhammad ibn Qays, from Muhammad ibn 'Ubayd Allah Abu 'Awn al-Thaqafi, from 'Umar and 'Ali, who said: “If he converts to Islam and has land, we remove the Jizyah from him and take its Kharaj (land tax) from him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ سَيَّارٍ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ، أَنَّ دِهْقَانًا أَسْلَمَ عَلَى عَهْدِ عَلِيٍّ، فَقَالَ لَهُ عَلِيٌّ: إِنْ أَقَمْتَ فِي أَرْضِكَ رَفَعْنَا الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ رَأْسِكَ، وَأَخَذْنَاهَا مِنْ أَرْضِكَ، وَإِنْ تَحَوَّلَتْ عَنْهَا، فَنَحْنُ أَحَقُّ بِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Sayyar → Al-Zubayr ibn 'Adi → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Sayyar, from Al-Zubayr ibn 'Adi, that a Dihqan (landowner) converted to Islam during the time of 'Ali. 'Ali said to him: “If you remain in your land, we remove the Jizyah from your head and take it from your land. If you move away from it, we are more entitled to it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، أَنَّ رَجُلَيْنِ، مِنْ أَهْلِ أُلَّيْسٍ أَسْلَمَا فِي عَهْدِ عُمَرَ، فَأَتَيَا عُمَرَ، فَأَخْبَرَاهُ بِإِسْلَامِهِمَا، فَكَتَبَ لَهُمَا إِلَى عُثْمَانَ بْنِ حُنَيْفٍ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ رُءُوسِهِمَا، وَأَنْ يَأْخُذَ الطِّسْقَ مِنْ أَرْضِيهِمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Husayn → Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Husayn that two men from Ullays converted to Islam during the time of 'Umar. They came to 'Umar and informed him of their conversion. He wrote for them to 'Uthman ibn Hunayf to remove the Jizyah from their heads and take the Tasq (tax) from their lands.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَنَّ دِهْقَانَةً مِنْ أَهْلِ نَهْرِ الْمَلِكِ أَسْلَمَتْ، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: ادْفَعُوا إِلَيْهَا أَرْضَهَا تُؤَدِّي عَنْهَا الْخَرَاجَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Qays ibn Muslim → Tariq ibn Shihab → Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us: Sufyan told us from Qays ibn Muslim, from Tariq ibn Shihab, that a female landowner from Nahr al-Malik converted to Islam. 'Umar said: “Give her land to her, and let her pay the Kharaj for it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَنَّ دِهْقَانَةً أَسْلَمَتْ مِنْ نَهْرِ الْمَلِكِ، فَكَتَبَ عُمَرُ أَنْ خَيِّرُوهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Hasan ibn Salih → Qays ibn Muslim → Tariq ibn Shihab → Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Hasan ibn Salih, from Qays ibn Muslim, from Tariq ibn Shihab, that a female landowner from Nahr al-Malik converted to Islam, so 'Umar wrote to give her the choice.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، أَنَّ الرَّفِيلَ دِهْقَانَ النَّهْرَيْنِ أَسْلَمَ، فَفَرَضَ لَهُ عُمَرُ فِي أَلْفَيْنِ، وَرَفَعَ عَنْ رَأْسِهِ الْجِزْيَةَ وَدَفَعَ إِلَيْهِ أَرْضَهُ يُؤَدِّي عَنْهَا الْخَرَاجَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Jabir → 'Amir → Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Jabir, from 'Amir, that Al-Rafil, the Dihqan of Al-Nahrayn, converted to Islam. 'Umar assigned him two thousand, removed the Jizyah from his head, and gave him his land to pay Kharaj for it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عُبَيْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عُمَرَ عَمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ مِنْ أَهْلِ السَّوَادِ، فَقَالَ: مَنْ أَسْلَمَ مِنْ أَهْلِ السَّوَادِ مِمَّنْ لَهُ ذِمَّةٌ، فَلَهُ أَرْضُهُ وَمَالُهُ، وَمَنْ أَسْلَمَ مِمَّنْ لَا ذِمَّةَ لَهُ، وَإِنَّمَا أَخَذَهُ عَنْوَةً، فَأَرْضُهُ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ، قَالَ عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ: قَرَأْتُ هَذَا فِي كِتَابِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman → Hasan → 'Ubayd Allah ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman told us from Hasan, who said: I asked 'Ubayd Allah ibn 'Umar about those who converted to Islam from the people of Al-Sawad. He said: “Whoever converts from the people of Al-Sawad who has a Dhidmah (protection pact), his land and wealth are his. And whoever converts who has no Dhidmah, and was taken by force, his land belongs to the Muslims.” 'Ubayd Allah said: I read this in the book of 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدٌ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَسْلَمَ الرَّجُلُ مِنْ أَهْلِ السَّوَادِ، ثُمَّ أَقَامَ فِي أَرْضِهِ أَخَذَ مِنْهُ الْخَرَاجَ، فَإِنْ خَرَجَ مِنْهَا لَمْ يُؤْخَذْ مِنْهُ الْخَرَاجُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Humayd → Hasan → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Humayd told us from Hasan, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: “If a man from the people of Al-Sawad converts to Islam and remains on his land, Kharaj is taken from him. But if he leaves it, Kharaj is not taken from him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّ مُكَاتَبًا لَهُ عَجَزَ، فَرَدَّهُ مَمْلُوكًا، وَأَمْسَكَ مَا أَخَذَ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → Muhammad ibn Ishaq → Nafi' → Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar: That a Mukatab of his was unable to pay, so he returned him to slavery and kept what he had taken from him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: لَهُمْ مَا أَخَذُوا مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Ash'ath → Abu al-Zubayr → Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs told us from Ash'ath, from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, who said: “They keep what they took from him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، وَوَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، قَالَ: يَجْعَلُهُ فِي مِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah / Waki' → Isma'il → Al-Sha'bi → Masruq
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah and Waki' told us from Isma'il, from Al-Sha'bi, from Masruq, who said: “He puts it in something similar.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يُنْظَرُ مَا كَانَ أَعَانَهُ النَّاسُ فِي مُكَاتَبَتِهِ، فَيَجْعَلُهُ فِي الرِّقَابِ، وَمَا كَانَ مِنْ كَسْبِهِ وَمَالِهِ فَهُوَ لِمَوْلَاهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: “Whatever people helped him with in his Mukatabah is looked at and put into freeing slaves, and whatever was from his earnings and wealth belongs to his master.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: يَجْعَلُهُ فِي مِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Hajjaj → Al-Hakam → Ibrahim → Shurayh
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs told us from Hajjaj, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, from Shurayh, who said: “He puts it in something similar.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: هُوَ لِمَوْلَاهُ. وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: يَجْعَلُهُ فِي الرِّقَابِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Hasan ibn Salih → Mujalid → Al-Sha'bi → Shurayh
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Hasan ibn Salih, from Mujalid, from Al-Sha'bi, from Shurayh, who said: “It belongs to his master.” And Sufyan said from Mughirah from Ibrahim: “He puts it into freeing slaves.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَرْوَانَ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا، حَثَّ النَّاسَ عَلَى ابْنِ النَّبَّاحِ، فَجَمَعُوا لَهُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ، فَفَضَلَتْ فَضْلَةٌ فَجَعَلَهَا عَلِيٌّ فِي الْمُكَاتِبِينَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Sufyan → Abu Ja'far → Ja'far ibn Abi Tharwan → 'Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us from Sufyan, from Abu Ja'far, from Ja'far ibn Abi Tharwan, that 'Ali encouraged people to help Ibn al-Nabbah. They collected more than his Mukatabah for him, and there was a surplus, so 'Ali put it towards [helping other] Mukatabs.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: مُكَاتَبٌ سَأَلَ فِي رَقَبَةٍ أَوْ رَقَبَتَيْنِ فَأُعْطِيَ عَطَاءً، فَلَمَّا كَثُرَ فِي عَيْنِ أَبِي مُوسَى مَا أُعْطِيَ أَمَرَ بِهِ وَبِمَا أُعْطِيَ فَأُدْخِلَ، ثُمَّ نَظَرَ الَّذِي سَأَلَ فِيهِ، فَأَعْطَاهُ إِيَّاهُ فَأَخَذَهُ الْفَضْلُ فَجَعَلَهُ فِي رَقَبَتِهِ أَوْ رِقَابٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn 'Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: A Mukatab asked for help for one or two slaves and was given a lot. When Abu Musa saw how much he was given, he ordered him and what he was given to be brought in. Then he looked at what he asked for and gave it to him, and took the surplus and put it towards freeing slaves.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ صَبِيحٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبَانَ، عَنْ صُبَيْحِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، حَثَّ النَّاسَ عَلَى مُكَاتَبِهِ، فَجَمَعُوا لَهُ فَأَدَّى مُكَاتَبَتَهُ، وَبَقِيَتْ فَضْلَةٌ فَجَعَلَهَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ فِي الْمُكَاتِبِينَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki' → Al-Rabi' ibn Sabih → Yazid ibn Aban → Subayh ibn 'Abd Allah → 'Abd Allah ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki' told us: Al-Rabi' ibn Sabih told us from Yazid ibn Aban, from Subayh ibn 'Abd Allah, that 'Abd Allah ibn 'Abbas encouraged people to help his Mukatab. They collected for him, and he paid his Mukatabah, and there was a surplus, so 'Abd Allah put it towards [helping other] Mukatabs.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِأَنَّ يُعَالِجَ الرَّجُلُ النَّخْلَ، وَيَقُومُ عَلَيْهِ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ مَا لَمْ يُنْفِقْ هُوَ مِنْهُ شَيْئًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abdah ibn Sulayman → Sa'id ibn Abi 'Arubah → Qatadah → Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abdah ibn Sulayman told us from Sa'id ibn Abi 'Arubah, from Qatadah, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, that he said: “There is no harm in a man tending to palm trees and taking care of them for a third or a quarter, as long as he does not spend anything on them himself.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ ذَلِكَ إِلَّا بِأَجْرٍ مَعْلُومٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abdah ibn Sulayman → Ibn Abi 'Arubah → Qatadah → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abdah ibn Sulayman told us from Ibn Abi 'Arubah, from Qatadah, from Al-Hasan: That he used to dislike that unless it was for a known wage.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنِ الْفُضَيْلِ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، قَالَ: النَّخْلُ يُعْطَى مَنْ عَمِلَ فِيهِ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn 'Ulayyah → Ayyub → Al-Fudayl → Salim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us from Ayyub, from Al-Fudayl, from Salim, who said: “The palm trees are given to the one who works on them from its produce.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ يَعْمَلُ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: “He used to dislike everything done for a third or a quarter.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يُسْتَأْجَرَ الْأَجِيرُ يَعْمَلُ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri: That he saw no harm in hiring a worker to work the land for a third or a quarter.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ "يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُسْتَأْجَرَ الْأَجِيرُ فَيَقُولُ: لَكَ ثُلُثٌ أَوْ رُبُعٌ مِمَّا يُخْرِجُ أَرْضِي هَذَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Shu'bah → Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ghundar told us from Shu'bah, from Hammad, who said: He used to dislike hiring a worker saying: “You have a third or a quarter of what this land of mine produces.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ مُحَمَّدًا عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَدْفَعُ إِلَى النَّسَّاجِ الثَّوْبَ بِالثُّلُثِ، وَدِرْهَمٍ أَوْ بِالرُّبُعِ أَوْ بِمَا تَرَاضَيَا عَلَيْهِ؟ فَقَالَ: لَا أَعْلَمُ بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Muhammad ibn Abi 'Adi → Ibn 'Awn → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Muhammad ibn Abi 'Adi told us from Ibn 'Awn, who said: I asked Muhammad about a man giving a weaver a garment for a third plus a dirham, or for a quarter, or whatever they agree upon? He said: “I do not know any harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وَالْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَدْفَعَ الرَّجُلُ الثَّوْبَ إِلَى النَّسَّاجِ بِالثُّلُثِ قَالَ: وَكَانَ عَطَاءُ لَا يَرَى بِذَلِكَ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn 'Ulayyah → Layth → Al-Sha'bi / Al-Hakam → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn 'Ulayyah told us from Layth, from Al-Sha'bi and Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim: That he disliked a man giving a garment to a weaver for a third. He said: And 'Ata' saw no harm in that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَدْفَعَهُ إِلَيْهِ بِالثُّلُثِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, who said: “There is no harm in giving it to him for a third.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ أَبِي هِلَالٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يَدْفَعَ الثَّوْبَ إِلَى النَّسَّاجِ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd ibn Hubab → Abu Hilal → Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Zayd ibn Hubab told us from Abu Hilal, from Qatadah: That he saw no harm in giving a garment to a weaver for a third or a quarter.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ مَرْوَانَ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ شَهْرَ بْنِ حَوْشَبٍ عَنِ الثَّوْبِ يَدْفَعُهُ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ إِلَى الْحَائِكِ، قَالَ: شَرْطٌ بِغَيْرٍ لَا غَيْرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd ibn Hubab → Marwan ibn Mu'awiyah → Shahr ibn Hawshab
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Zayd ibn Hubab told us from Marwan ibn Mu'awiyah, who said: I asked Shahr ibn Hawshab about giving a garment to a weaver for a third or a quarter. He said: “A condition other than [just] that.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُدْفَعَ الثَّوْبُ إِلَى الْحَائِكِ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd ibn Hubab → Mubarak → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Zayd ibn Hubab told us from Mubarak, from Al-Hasan: That he disliked giving a garment to a weaver for a third or a quarter.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَيُّوبَ، وَيَعْلَى بْنَ حَكِيمٍ، عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَدْفَعُ الثَّوْبَ إِلَى النَّسَّاجِ بِالثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ، فَلَمْ يَرَيَا بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Hammad ibn Zayd → Ayyub / Ya'la ibn Hakim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi told us from Hammad ibn Zayd, who said: I asked Ayyub and Ya'la ibn Hakim about a man giving a garment to a weaver for a third or a quarter, and they saw no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: ذَكَرُوا الرَّجُلَ يَضْطَرُّ إِلَى الْمَيْتَةِ، وَإِلَى مَالِ الرَّجُلِ الْمُسْلِمِ، فَقُلْتُ: يَأْكُلُ الْمَيْتَةَ وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ دِينَارٍ: يَأْكُلُ مَالَ الرَّجُلِ الْمُسْلِمِ، فَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيِّبِ: أَصَبْتَ، إِنَّ الْمَيْتَةَ تَحِلُّ لَهُ إِذَا اضْطُرَّ، وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ مَالُ الْمُسْلِمِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi Za'idah → 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi Za'idah told us from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam, from his father, who said: They mentioned a man compelled to eat carrion or the wealth of a Muslim. I said: “He eats the carrion.” 'Abd Allah ibn Dinar said: “He eats the wealth of the Muslim.” Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib said: “You are correct; carrion becomes lawful for him if compelled, but the wealth of a Muslim does not become lawful for him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا اضْطُرَّ إِلَى مَا حُرِّمَ عَلَيْهِ، فَمَا حُرِّمَ عَلَيْهِ فَهُوَ لَهُ حَلَالٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Jabir → Abu Ja'far
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Sharik told us from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far, who said: “If he is compelled to what is forbidden to him, then what is forbidden to him becomes lawful for him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: مَنْ بَاعَ حُبْلَى أَوْ أَعْتَقَهَا وَاسْتَثْنَى مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا، قَالَ: لَهُ ثُنْيَاهُ فِيمَا قَدِ اسْتَبَانَ خَلْقَهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَسْتَبِنْ خَلْقُهُ فَلَا شَيْءَ لَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: Whoever sells a pregnant woman or frees her and makes an exception for what is in her womb. He said: “His exception is valid if its form is evident, but if its form is not evident, he has nothing.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُجِيزُ ثُنْيَاهُ فِي الْبَيْعِ، وَلَا يُجِيزُهَا فِي الْعِتْقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym told us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan: That he used to validate his exception in sale but not in manumission.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَعْتِقُ الْأَمَةَ، وَيَسْتَثْنِي مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا، قَالَ: لَهُ ثُنْيَاهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sa'id → Hisham → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn Sa'id told us from Hisham, from Muhammad, regarding a man who frees a slave woman and makes an exception for what is in her womb. He said: “His exception is valid.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: هُمَا حُرَّانِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → 'Abd al-A'la → Ma'mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr said: 'Abd al-A'la told us from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, who said: “They are both free.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانَ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَعَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وَعَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالُوا: لَهُ ثُنْيَاهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Yaman → Sufyan → Ibn Jurayj / Jabir / Mansur → 'Ata' / Al-Sha'bi / Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn Yaman told us from Sufyan, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata'; and from Sufyan, from Jabir, from Al-Sha'bi; and from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who all said: “His exception is valid.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَرَمِيُّ بْنُ عُمَارَةَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، وَحَمَّادًا عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَعْتِقُ الْأَمَةَ، وَيَسْتَثْنِي مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا، قَالَا: لَهُ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Harami ibn 'Umarah → Shu'bah → Al-Hakam / Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Harami ibn 'Umarah told us from Shu'bah, who said: I asked Al-Hakam and Hammad about a man who frees a slave woman and makes an exception for what is in her womb. They said: “He has that right.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا قُرَّةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ فَضَاءٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَعْتِقُ الْأَمَةَ، وَيَسْتَثْنِي مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا، قَالَ: لَهُ ثُنْيَاهُ
